localizations.title: Hollywood Confidential
localizations.hint_2: This spent .38 caliber bullet casing suggests the police came to arrest Marlowe.
localizations.hint_4: Marlowe's disappearance may be linked to one of his investigations. There could be a lead to follow in the roll of film inside the camera that may help you find him. What could you do to confirm this theory?
localizations.hint_7_first: There's a way you can sometimes recover information written on the previous page of a notepad.
localizations.hint_7: When writing on a notepad, the pressure from the pen leaves an indentation on the page underneath. With the correct tool, you should be able to reveal the information you seek.
localizations.hint_8: A piece of paper is stuck in the typewriter. It covers one of the keys.
localizations.hint_9: Unless you can prove these men committed a crime, you should leave them alone.
localizations.hint_11_first: Based on the anonymous letter (1) sent to Irina Nevski, Moretti's men often communicate by cutting out words and letters from newspapers. This newspaper was probably used to write another message to the Russian princess. First, deduce which words have been cut out from the newspaper, then place them in the correct order to recreate the message.
localizations.hint_11: Going from top to bottom, left to right, the letters cut out from the newspaper are: HOLLYWOOD, Y, MOVIE, THE, REEL, THE, IS, BURIED, SIGN, UNDER, OF, THE.
localizations.hint_12_first: The initials on the piece of paper are those of Jessica Klissman, the lead actress in the stolen movie reel, and who was also seen with Charles Forest in the pictures. The rest of the note indicates her apartment number.
localizations.hint_12: The apartment number (33) is written in blue. You can combine it with a red card.
localizations.hint_14: According to the plane ticket, coupled with the note Charles Forest left, the two lovers plan to flee the country and go to Mexico. Where do you think they had arranged to meet?
localizations.hint_16: In a few moments, she will be done with her song. Turn up the volume on your device and wait for the show to end.
localizations.hint_19: What if you developed the roll of film?
localizations.hint_23_first: Look at these pictures closely. One of them gives a lead to find the people Marlowe was investigating.
localizations.hint_23: You can make out the name of the location where one of these shots was taken.
localizations.hint_26_first: Which terminal and gate should you go to in order to intercept Charles Forest?
localizations.hint_26: Notice the terminal and gate numbers on Jessica's ticket (14). They are written in red…
localizations.hint_35: You notice something behind the police officer.
localizations.hint_36: You won't get anything else out of the officer, but if you carefully examine the rest of his office (35) you may find some important information.
localizations.hint_37_first: Recreate the instructions leading you to Villa Nevski using the piece of paper stuck in the typewriter's keyboard.
localizations.hint_37: Draw the route by following the instructions. Its shape will lead you to Villa Nevski.
localizations.hint_44_first: One of these men is probably Charles Forest. You should have noticed an important detail about his outfit earlier in the adventure.
localizations.hint_44: Look again at the last frame of the film you found in the editing room (96).
localizations.hint_46: Don't give up now! It's just a door…
localizations.hint_49: The words that Charles Forest wrote to Moretti incriminate the Godfather and his men in this shady case.
localizations.hint_53: Does this flyer actually belong to your boss? Look at the location written on it if you feel like pursuing this lead.
localizations.hint_54: Now you can go to Charles Forest's studio and investigate there.
localizations.hint_55: She may have helpful information about the people Marlowe was investigating.
localizations.hint_57: You hold compromising information about her husband. If you give her this information, she may be willing to assist in your investigation.
localizations.hint_63: According to information given to you by the singer (78), the man in the pictures (23) may have been featured in Hollywood Nightlife. If this is the case, the tabloid's secretary may be able to disclose his identity.
localizations.hint_64: A detective named Finnegan was assigned to the case involving your boss, but the case was passed on to the FBI (who will undoubtably refuse to tell you anything about Marlowe).
localizations.hint_66: You're not impressed by Lieutenant Finnegan's lame explanation for his injuries. You need to find a way to make him talk.
localizations.hint_69_first: Based on the anonymous letter (1) sent to Irina Nevski, Moretti's men often communicate by cutting out words and letters from newspapers. This newspaper was probably used to write another message to the Russian princess. First, deduce which words have been cut out from the newspaper, then place them in the correct order to recreate the message.
localizations.hint_69: Going from top to bottom, left to right, the letters cut out from the newspaper are: HOLLYWOOD, Y, MOVIE, THE, REEL, THE, IS, BURIED, SIGN, UNDER, OF, THE.
localizations.hint_71: He's not going to let you in without getting something in return.
localizations.hint_78: In order to identify the producer, you need to find the tabloid in which he may have been featured.
localizations.hint_81: In a few moments, she will be done with her song. Turn up the volume on your device and wait for the show to end.
localizations.hint_83: Is Marlowe's disappeareance linked in any way to communists? Something seems shady, but still, you can try to visit the warehouse by the docks on Fairbanks Street.
localizations.hint_86: You now know the identity of the man in the pictures: Charles Forest. The secretary gives you two further leads to follow so that you can keep investigating.
localizations.hint_88: So, it turns out that the communist flyer you found at Marlowe's was just false evidence - a red herring!
localizations.hint_91_first: During your investigation, you have seen what kind of books Jessica liked to read.
localizations.hint_91: During the shooting of the film, Jessica had a book with her…
localizations.hint_phonebook_journal: According to the singer (78), the man in the pictures was featured in a local tabloid. Only one of the 4 newspapers listed seems to publish rumors and gossip.
localizations.hint_phonebook_finnegan: If Finnegan is indeed the cop who abducted your boss at the beginning of the adventure, you know his first name.
localizations.hint_blueroom_door: Why are you standing in front of this door?
localizations.answer_hint_2: To get the address of the police precinct, use the dedicated phone Book button and look for POLICE.
localizations.answer_hint_4: Use the dedicated Phone Book button and enter "PHOTOGRAPH" to find a store likely to develop the roll of film inside the camera.
localizations.answer_hint_7: Use your pencil (42) to lightly darken the notepad (7).
localizations.answer_hint_8: The piece of paper covers the "S" key. Remove it.
localizations.answer_hint_11: When placed in the correct order, the words form the following message: "THE MOVIE REEL IS BURIED UNDER THE Y OF THE HOLLYWOOD SIGN". Look for "HOLLYWOOD" in the Phone Book.
localizations.answer_hint_12: If you haven't already done so, look for "JESSICA KLISSMAN" in the Phone Book. Once at her building (13), go to her apartment (33).
localizations.answer_hint_14: Charles Forest is probably at the airport waiting for his unfortunate mistress. Look for "AIRPORT" in the Phone Book.
localizations.answer_hint_19: Have the roll of film (4) developed at this photography store (19).
localizations.answer_hint_23: You can see BLUE ROOM in the upper left of the picture. Look for "BLUE ROOM" in the Phone Book to locate the club and continue your investigation.
localizations.answer_hint_26: To find Charles Forest in the airport (26), go to Terminal 2, Gate 16 (+18).
localizations.answer_hint_30: Show the communist flyer (53) to the old man (30).
localizations.answer_hint_35: Take notice of the hidden number behind the police officer.
localizations.answer_hint_36: In the office (35), take notice of the hidden number behind the police officer.
localizations.answer_hint_37: Follow the handwritten instructions (37 and S) to draw the route. This should write out the numbers 5 and 7.
localizations.answer_hint_44: Notice the hat on Forest's seat; it has 2 gray stripes. Select the man wearing the same hat to detain him.
localizations.answer_hint_46: Use your strength (22) to force open the door (46).
localizations.answer_hint_49: Now that you have tangible proof (49) of Moretti's involvement, return to the BLUE ROOM to confront him and his gang (9) about the theft.
localizations.answer_hint_53: A communist flyer that slipped between the cushions could cause problems for Marlowe. It looks like someone planted it here. If you wish to follow this lead, use the Phone Book to find "PHIL'S DINER".
localizations.answer_hint_54: Show the pass (54) to the watchman controlling access to Forest Movies Studios (20).
localizations.answer_hint_55: Show the photographs you have developed (23) to the singer (55) to interrogate her about the people depicted in them.
localizations.answer_hint_57: Show Irina Nevski (57) the pictures documenting her husband's infidelity (23).
localizations.answer_hint_63: Show the secretary (63) the pictures that Marlowe (23) took of the couple.
localizations.answer_hint_64: Don't waste your time with the FBI; they won't talk to you about Marlowe's case. You'd better try finding information about the detective, Finnegan. Look for "FINNEGAN" in the Phone Book.
localizations.answer_hint_66: Perhaps you can use force (22) to coerce Lieutenant Finnegan into spilling the beans.
localizations.answer_hint_67: The name of the woman Charles Forest is spending time with is Jess, according to the singer (78). She is probably the actress who perfomred in the latest movie Charles Forest produced: JESSICA KLISSMAN. Look for her name in the Phone Book.
localizations.answer_hint_69: When placed in the correct order, the words form the following message: "THE MOVIE REEL IS BURIED UNDER THE Y OF THE HOLLYWOOD SIGN". Drag the cursor featuring a shovel under the letter Y to dig there.
localizations.answer_hint_71: You'll need to grease the bouncer's palm (71) to get inside. Give him the ten dollar bill (10).
localizations.answer_hint_78: To find a newspaper, use the dedicated Phone Book button and enter "NEWSPAPER".
localizations.answer_hint_81: Wait for the music to end, and then look at what appears on your screen.
localizations.answer_hint_83: Don't waste your time investigating the docks. It is not a reliable lead.
localizations.answer_hint_86: To investigate Charles Forest's workplace (FOREST MOVIES) and his wife (IRINA NEVSKI), look for these names in the Phone Book.
localizations.answer_hint_88: Don't bother with the lead presented by the communist flyer (53) found in Marlowe's office. Lieutenant Finnegan left it there to frame Marlowe and put an end to his investigation.
localizations.answer_hint_91: Carefully examine the last frame of the film you found in the editing room (96). On Jessica Klissman's chair is Patricia Highsmith's book, Strangers on a Train. Select this book on the bookshelf.
localizations.answer_hint_phonebook_finnegan: On the introduction card, at the end of the conversation you had with Marlowe over the phone, he mentioned the name "SAM". Select "SAM FINNEGAN".
localizations.answer_hint_blueroom_door: Knock three times at the door to continue.
localizations.machine_44: You grab the man by the shoulder. He turns around, surprised and frightened. It's Forest, all right!
localizations.machine_58: You hear police sirens approaching! The mobsters run away, but as you are about to flee as well, you spot something on the ground.
localizations.machine_69: The soil seems looser here. You dig and find the reel you have been searching for! CONGRATULATIONS! Princess Nevski is delighted, and she rewards you handomely. She can finally get rid of her awful husband and divorce him. With her help, you give the FBI all the evidence they need to clear Marlowe. Thanks to you, he is a free man. Back at your shabby office, the phone starts ringing again. You’re back in business!
localizations.machine_91: You grab the book and look inside.
localizations.phonebook_photo: You find a photography store in the Phone Book. You jot down the address and set off for its location.
localizations.phonebook_blueroom: In the Phone Book, you find the address for a club called The Blue Room. At nightfall, you arrive, and even from the outside you can hear the music coming from within.
localizations.phonebook_journal: You set off for the offices of Hollywood Night Life at daybreak.
localizations.phonebook_forestmovies: You're off to Forest Movies Studios.
localizations.phonebook_nevski: You look in the Phone Book for Irina Nevski's address. There is nothing listed under that name, but stuck between two pages you find a torn handwritten note mentioning her.
localizations.phonebook_klissman: Ms. Klissman's name is indeed in the Phone Book. You make your way to the indicated address.
localizations.phonebook_airport: You hail a cab and tell him to drive as fast as possible to the airport.
localizations.phonebook_hollywood: You climb the southen slope of Mount Lee and reach the base of the letters making up the legendary Hollywood sign. You are near the top, out of breath, with a spectacular overlook of the city.
localizations.phonebook_police: You find the central police precinct for the LAPD in the Phone Book. You go there to gather some information.
localizations.phonebook_finnegan: You write down Sam Finnegan's address and head to his place.
localizations.phonebook_armurier: "Ah, yes, it's a .38 caliber, used exclusively by the police force. I can't sell you any."
localizations.phonebook_diner: Phil's Diner is a small dive located near the edge of the city. You go and have a look around.
localizations.phonebook_docks: The docks are in full swing. After awhile, you finally find the warehouse the old man told you about, but it seems to be deserted. Looks like this was a false lead. What a waste of time!
localizations.phonebook_gare: You hide in front of the train station and see a bunch of people who have nothing to do with this case.
localizations.phonebook_fbi: After arriving at the FBI offices, you take a seat in the waiting room, and wait for quite some time. They take your fingerprints, but ultimately tell you nothing about Marlowe, and kindly (but firmly) ask you to get out of there. What a waste of time!
localizations.phonebook_tooearly: There is nothing to do here right now.
localizations.blueroom_door:
You knock at the dressing room door.
 After a brief silence, a voice invites you in.
localizations.phonebook_options: There are several entries for this keyword:
localizations.phonebook_instructions: In this adventure, the Phone Book will allow you to move from one location to another. To do so, enter one or more keywords (name, profession, etc.); Then, if there are releant matches, confirm to find out which card to take. Be careful! Some locations are a waste of time.
localizations.machine_58_instructions: All players should participate by using at least one finger. Shoot at the mobsters within the allotted time by tapping them on your screen. Avoid shooting at passers-by.
localizations.machine_blueroom_desc: The singer leaves the stage amidst thunderous applause. You follow her discreetly to her dressing room.
